Title: Raspberry Ketone Accumulation in Nicotiana benthamiana and Saccharomyces cerevisiae by Expression of Fused Pathway Genes
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type N. benthamiana; H4590/H5304-derived yeast strains
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Formation of benzalacetone/raspberry ketone branch from p-coumaroyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form 4-hydroxybenzalacetone, the direct precursor of raspberry ketone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as a key control step for productive flux when fused to 4CL
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural flavor/fragrance compound and health-promoting supplement
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: N. benthamiana leaves (mainly glucosides); S. cerevisiae culture medium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous transient expression in tobacco leaves; constitutive engineered expression in yeast fermentation strains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes raspberry ketone production; natural RpBAS outperformed RpBAS(S331V) in both tobacco and yeast, especially when fused to 4CL
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; heterologous pathway reconstruction in S. cerevisiae; gene fusion design; metabolite profiling by GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration in tobacco; exogenous p-coumaric acid feeding in yeast; 7-day fermentation; possible cytosolic pH effects discussed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RpBAS alone or in separate constructs produced only trace to very low raspberry ketone in N. benthamiana. 2. Fusion of 4CL to natural RpBAS dramatically increased raspberry ketone glucoside accumulation in tobacco leaves to about 33.5 μg/g DW without added RiZS1 and to 11.3 μg/g DW with RiZS1. 3. In yeast, natural RpBAS fused to Pc4CL gave the highest titers, 61 mg/L with p-HCA feeding and 47 mg/L in de novo strains, clearly exceeding the S331V mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SES synthetic constitutive promoter/bidirectional SES promoters
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highly useful for metabolic engineering of natural raspberry ketone production; prioritize natural BAS fused to 4CL rather than S331V mutant; suitable for plant transient production and yeast cell factories
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Accumulation of rhododendrol side product; production plateau in de novo yeast strains; possible sensitivity of BAS(S331V) to cellular pH reduces performance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper indicates many organisms possess endogenous reductase activity converting 4-hydroxybenzalacetone to raspberry ketone, so BAS does not complete the final reduction step. Natural RpBAS unexpectedly outperformed the reported S331V mutant under the tested conditions.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type N. benthamiana; H4590/H5304-derived yeast strains
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Formation of benzalacetone/raspberry ketone branch from p-coumaroyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Engineered BAS variant intended to synthesize 4-hydroxybenzalacetone from p-coumaroyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established as superior; inferior to natural BAS in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural flavor/fragrance compound and health-promoting supplement
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: N. benthamiana leaves; S. cerevisiae culture medium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous transient expression in tobacco leaves; constitutive engineered expression in yeast fermentation strains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports raspberry ketone production but is markedly less effective than natural RpBAS under the tested plant and yeast conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; heterologous pathway reconstruction in S. cerevisiae; fusion with 4CL; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration; exogenous p-HCA feeding; 7-day fermentation; performance may depend on cytosolic pH
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. At4CL-BAS(S331V) plus RiZS1 increased raspberry ketone glucoside in tobacco only to about 0.9 μg/g DW. 2. In yeast, Pc4CL with RpBAS(S331V) produced 4.3 mg/L raspberry ketone with p-HCA feeding, and RiZS1 plus 4CL-BAS(S331V) produced 5.6 mg/L. 3. In de novo yeast strains, the S331V version yielded only 6.3 mg/L, far below the natural BAS version.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SES synthetic constitutive promoter/bidirectional SES promoters
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority engineering choice than natural BAS for raspberry ketone production in plants and yeast under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower productivity than natural BAS; proposed pH sensitivity may impair activity in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion suggests the S331V mutant may be inefficient because of pH dependence, substrate specificity, or structural constraints.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Activation of p-coumaric acid to p-coumaroyl-CoA precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates p-coumaric acid to p-coumaroyl-CoA using CoA and ATP
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for channeling precursor to BAS when fused
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural flavor/fragrance compound and health-promoting supplement
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: N. benthamiana leaves, mainly as raspberry ketone glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous transient expression in infiltrated leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly enhances raspberry ketone accumulation in tobacco when fused to RpBAS; separate expression only slight increase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression and fusion construct comparison in N. benthamiana; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration; leaf tissue; 6 days after infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. At4CL expression together with RpBAS in separate constructs gave only a slight increase in raspberry ketone glucoside. 2. Fusion of At4CL1 with natural RpBAS caused a major increase to 33.5 μg/g DW in tobacco leaves. 3. The effect supports substrate channeling of p-coumaroyl-CoA from 4CL to BAS.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent engineering target for fusion-enzyme design to direct p-coumaroyl-CoA away from competing flavonoid/lignin pathways toward raspberry ketone
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competition with endogenous flavonoid and lignin biosynthesis for coumaroyl-CoA is implied
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Coumaroyl-CoA is highlighted as a highly regulated branch precursor in plants; fusion to BAS likely minimizes loss to competing pathways.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Activation of p-coumaric acid to p-coumaroyl-CoA precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates p-coumaric acid to p-coumaroyl-CoA for entry into raspberry ketone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for precursor channeling when fused to BAS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural flavor/fragrance compound and health-promoting supplement
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: constitutive engineered expression in yeast strains with fed and de novo pathway configurations
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables raspberry ketone production in yeast from exogenous or de novo p-coumaric acid, with best performance when fused to natural RpBAS
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway reconstruction with p-HCA feeding and de novo pathway assembly; comparison of separate vs fused designs; GC-MS profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous p-HCA feeding or de novo p-coumaric acid supply from glucose; 7-day fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pc4CL with RpBAS(S331V) enabled 4.3 mg/L raspberry ketone in fed yeast. 2. Fusion Pc4CL-RpBAS with RiZS1 produced 61 mg/L raspberry ketone with p-HCA feeding, the highest reported in yeast according to the paper. 3. In de novo strains, Pc4CL fused to natural BAS supported 47 mg/L raspberry ketone from glucose.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core enzyme for yeast raspberry ketone cell factories; fusion to natural BAS is strongly supported for maximizing flux from p-HCA to product
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can contribute to unwanted phloretic acid formation from p-coumaroyl-CoA in yeast background metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The Discussion notes endogenous yeast metabolism can divert p-coumaroyl-CoA to phloretic acid, highlighting a metabolic loss route downstream of 4CL activity.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type N. benthamiana; H4590/H5304-derived yeast strains
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final reduction step from benzalacetone intermediate to raspberry ketone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; dispensable or nonbeneficial under the tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural flavor/fragrance compound and health-promoting supplement
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: N. benthamiana leaves; S. cerevisiae culture medium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous transient expression in tobacco leaves; constitutive engineered expression in yeast strains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributed little or negatively to raspberry ketone accumulation in tobacco and was not required for substantial product formation; included in yeast strains but endogenous reductases may substitute
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression tests in N. benthamiana; heterologous pathway reconstruction in S. cerevisiae; GC-MS profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RiZS1 coexpressed with RpBAS in tobacco yielded only trace raspberry ketone glucoside. 2. Coexpression with At4CL-BAS did not improve and even reduced production relative to At4CL-BAS alone in tobacco. 3. In yeast pathway constructs RiZS1 was included, but the paper concludes many organisms have endogenous BAR activity and RiZS1 is not essential for raspberry ketone formation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SES synthetic constitutive promoter/bidirectional SES promoters
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Optional rather than essential engineering component; may be omitted or carefully tested depending on host background reductase capacity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible cosuppression or interference with endogenous reductases in plants is proposed; added pathway burden without clear benefit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses that many organisms inherently possess enzymes with BAR activity, explaining why raspberry ketone can be produced without RiZS1.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: De novo p-coumaric acid supply for raspberry ketone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts tyrosine to p-coumaric acid by ammonia elimination
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enabling step for de novo pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural flavor/fragrance compound and health-promoting supplement
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: engineered constitutive expression in de novo yeast strains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled de novo raspberry ketone production from glucose by generating p-coumaric acid precursor in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome integration of FjTAL into yeast de novo pathway strains; fermentation without external p-HCA; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glucose as carbon source; 7-day fermentation without precursor feeding
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ARO7G141S; ARO4(K229L); Pc4CL; RpBAS or RpBAS(S331V)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FjTAL was incorporated into a de novo p-coumaric acid-producing yeast chassis together with deregulated aromatic-pathway genes. 2. This enabled subsequent raspberry ketone biosynthesis from glucose. 3. De novo strains produced up to 47 mg/L raspberry ketone when paired with natural BAS fusion constructs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important enabling step for precursor-self-sufficient yeast production systems, removing dependence on exogenous p-HCA feeding
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May increase flux into related aromatic byproducts when aromatic pathway is deregulated
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen because it was described as a highly efficient TAL gene for p-coumaric acid production.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural flavor/fragrance compound and health-promoting supplement
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Precursor supply through the shikimate/aromatic amino acid pathway
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Flux from chorismate toward aromatic amino acid biosynthesis and ultimately tyrosine-derived p-coumaric acid supply
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Feedback-insensitive ARO7G141S increases pathway flux toward aromatic precursor formation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important flux-enhancing component enabling higher de novo p-coumaric acid and raspberry ketone production
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: normally aromatic amino acid feedback; engineered mutation removes sensitivity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: engineered constitutive expression in de novo yeast strains
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances de novo raspberry ketone biosynthesis by increasing precursor flux in aromatic amino acid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome engineering of de novo yeast strains combined with downstream raspberry ketone quantification by GC-MS
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Feedback-insensitive ARO7G141S was introduced into de novo yeast strains to channel metabolism toward p-coumaric acid. 2. Combined with FjTAL and ARO4(K229L), it supported de novo raspberry ketone production. 3. The deregulated aromatic-pathway background enabled titers up to 47 mg/L with natural BAS fusion.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful metabolic engineering target for precursor amplification in microbial production systems
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May elevate off-target aromatic metabolites such as phenylethyl alcohol via broader shikimate pathway effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzymatic, in this paper ARO7 is used primarily as a flux-regulating, feedback-insensitive metabolic control node rather than as the direct raspberry ketone pathway enzyme.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural flavor/fragrance compound and health-promoting supplement
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Entry flux into the shikimate pathway and aromatic precursor supply
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Carbon flux into aromatic amino acid biosynthesis feeding tyrosine and p-coumaric acid production
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Feedback-insensitive ARO4(K229L) increases precursor flux into the aromatic pathway
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important upstream flux-control component for de novo raspberry ketone biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: normally tyrosine feedback; engineered mutation removes sensitivity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: present in engineered yeast background H5304 and downstream strains
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports increased de novo raspberry ketone production by enhancing shikimate-pathway flux to aromatic precursors
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of ARO4(K229L)-containing yeast chassis for de novo pathway construction and product quantification by GC-MS
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARO4(K229L) was part of the engineered yeast background used for de novo raspberry ketone production. 2. The mutation was intended to channel the shikimate pathway toward increased p-coumaric acid precursor formation. 3. The deregulated background contributed to successful raspberry ketone production from glucose.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: engineered background expression cassette in strain H5304
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful chassis modification for microbial production of phenylpropanoid-derived compounds
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broader aromatic-pathway deregulation may increase side products such as phenylethyl alcohol
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzymatic, in this study ARO4 is presented as an upstream flux-deregulating control element for precursor overproduction.
